raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased a XXXX lawn mower from XXXX for {$420.00} plus tax using my American Express Everyday card. On first use, the mower smoked heavily and appeared defective. XXXX refused to accept a return, citing their policy against returns on gas-powered equipment once fuel has been added even though adding gas is necessary to test the product. 

I pointed out that other major retailers, like XXXX XXXX and XXXX, accept returns on defective gas-powered equipment even if fuel was added. I contacted the manufacturer, but the only authorized service center was over 50 miles away with a XXXX week wait. 

I filed a dispute with Amex shortly after. It was denied on XX/XX/year>, again after supervisor review on XX/XX/XXXX, and a third time on XX/XX/XXXX. Throughout the process, I supplied a detailed timeline, email correspondence, and photo and video evidence showing the defect and my efforts to resolve the issue.

I asked to escalate the matter to a higher department ( such as Executive Relations or Customer Advocacy ) and was told no such department exists. Im now left with a defective product, no resolution from the merchant, and no support from my card issuer despite multiple documented attempts.
